text: This is the VOA Special English Agriculture Report.
zh: [0] 这里是美国之音特别英语农业报道节目。

text: A discovery in a laboratory could help lead to a new generation of drought -resistant crops.
zh: [1] 实验室里的一项发现或将促成新一代抗旱作物的诞生。

text: Drought conditions cause plants to produce a stress hormone called abscisic acid.
zh: [2] 干旱条件会促使植物产生一种叫脱落酸的应激激素。

text: This chemical activates a set of protein molecules called receptors.
zh: [3] 这种化学物质能激活一组名为受体的蛋白质分子。

text: These receptors then activate a series of changes to help the plant survive.
zh: [4] 这些受体随后引发一系列变化，帮助植物存活下来。

text: The natural reaction of the receptors is to close so -called guard cells on and inside the leaves.
zh: [5] 受体的天然反应是关闭叶片表面及内部所谓的保卫细胞。

text: That closure decreases water loss
zh: [6] 这种关闭能减少水分流失，

text: and stops the plant from growing to save water during a drought.
zh: [7] 并使植物停止生长，从而在干旱期间节约水分。

text: Researchers say they have discovered a way to supercharge or increase this reaction.
zh: [8] 研究人员称他们发现了一种能强化或增强这一反应的方法。

text: Sean Cutler at the University of California Riverside led the team.
zh: [9] 加州大学河滨分校的肖恩·卡特勒（Sean Cutler）领导了这个研究小组。

text: The scientists engineered abscisic acid receptors that can be turned on at will and stay on.
zh: [10] 科学家们设计出可以随时开启并保持开启状态的脱落酸受体。

text: They tested hundreds of versions of of engineered receptor genes until they found ones that worked right together.
zh: [11] 他们测试了数百种改造过的受体基因，直到找到能够协同工作的版本。

text: The team worked with Arabidopsis, a plant often used in experiments.
zh: [12] 该团队使用拟南芥进行实验，这是一种常用于实验的植物。

text: The results recently appeared in the proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences.
zh: [13] 研究结果最近发表在《美国国家科学院院刊》上。

text: Professor Cutler says testing the new receptors in the field may take several years.
zh: [14] 卡特勒教授说，在田间测试这些新受体可能需要数年时间。

text: He also points out that drought is not the only cause of plant stress.
zh: [15] 他还指出，干旱并不是植物产生应激反应的唯一原因。

text: What would be stressful for a crop plant is not stressful for a cactus, for example,
zh: [16] 例如，对农作物来说会构成压力的条件，对仙人掌却不会构成压力，

text: because the cactus has evolved to live in dry deserts. In another development,
zh: [17] 因为仙人掌已经进化到能在干旱沙漠中生存。另据报道，

text: scientists have produced a new system for analyzing genetic markers in rice plants.
zh: [18] 科学家们研发出一种分析水稻基因标记的新系统。

text: A genetic marker is a DNA sequence with a known location.
zh: [19] 基因标记是指位置已知的一段DNA序列。

text: It can help scientists identify nearby genes linked to individual qualities or traits.
zh: [20] 它可以帮助科学家识别与特定性状或特征相关的邻近基因。

text: Anna McClung and Georgia Isinga are genetic scientists with the United States Department of Agriculture.
zh: [21] 安娜·麦克朗（Anna McClung）和乔治亚·伊辛加（Georgia Isinga）是美国农业部的遗传学家。

text: Their new system will let researchers genetically fingerprint rice varieties
zh: [22] 他们研发的新系统将使研究人员能够对水稻品种进行基因指纹分析，

text: and gain a better understanding of the markers.
zh: [23] 从而更好地理解这些基因标记。

text: Until now, breeders have been limited to using perhaps 200 markers.
zh: [24] 此前，育种人员最多只能使用约200个标记。

text: workers. But modern technology
zh: [25] 工作者们。但现代技术

text: lets scientists identify differences throughout the plant's genome, its genetic map.
zh: [26] 使科学家能够识别整个植物基因组（即其基因图谱）中的差异。

text: Ms. McClung says that means the ability to identify new genes that control biological pathways.
zh: [27] 麦克朗女士说，这意味着人们能够识别出控制生物通路的新基因。

text: These pathways in the plant may control traits like yield, disease resistance, and nutritional quality.
zh: [28] 植物体内的这些通路可能控制产量、抗病性和营养品质等性状。

text: The finding could make it easier in the future
zh: [29] 这一发现未来或将使农民更容易

text: for farmers to grow designer rice, rice bred to meet their specific needs.
zh: [30] 种植定制水稻，即根据自身特定需求培育的水稻品种。

text: For VOA Special English, I'm Alex Villareal. Read, listen, and learn English and
zh: [31] 我是亚历克斯·维拉里尔（Alex Villareal），为您带来美国之音特别英语节目。

text: much more with our programs and and activities at voaspecialenglish .com.
zh: [32] 欢迎访问voaspecialenglish.com，通过我们的节目和活动阅读、收听并学习英语，获取更多内容。
